name: Caddy
description: Automatic HTTPS reverse proxy. Routes clean URLs to all your services with zero cert management.

preflight_checks.description: Port 80 must be available for Caddy HTTP
Port 443 must be available for Caddy HTTPS

steps.instruction: ⚠ IMPORTANT: For HTTPS to work, you MUST complete DNS setup FIRST. Go to your DNS provider (Cloudflare, Namecheap, etc.) → DNS Records → Add TWO records: (1) A record: Name = @, Value = your server's PUBLIC IP. (2) A record: Name = * (wildcard), Value = same PUBLIC IP, TTL = Auto. Find your public IP at https://ifconfig.me
If using Cloudflare: make sure the proxy status (orange cloud) is set to 'DNS only' (grey cloud) for the wildcard record. Caddy handles TLS itself — Cloudflare proxy will break it

Verify DNS is working: open a terminal and run 'nslookup homepage.yourdomain.com'. It should return your server's public IP. If it shows a different IP or fails, wait 5 minutes for DNS propagation and try again
Your router/firewall must forward ports 80 and 443 to this server's local IP. Without port forwarding, Let's Encrypt cannot verify your domain and HTTPS will fail silently

Once DNS and port forwarding are configured, Caddy will automatically request and renew free Let's Encrypt TLS certificates. First certificate takes ~30 seconds. After that, access any service at https://appname.yourdomain.com
Without DNS/port forwarding: all services are still accessible at http://SERVER_IP:PORT directly. HTTPS is a bonus, not a requirement
